skill.name: pdf-processor
skill.description: Perform basic local PDF operations (merge, split, extract pages/text/tables, create) when users request offline PDF processing without external services.
skill.summary: Merge, split, extract text and tables from PDF files locally without cloud APIs.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 61 static analysis findings were reviewed and determined to be false positives. The 44 shell execution flags are markdown code block examples showing CLI usage, not actual shell execution. The 4 file write operations in pdf_tool.py are legitimate PDF file operations for the tool's core purpose. The 5 weak cryptographic algorithm alerts and 5 system reconnaissance flags are scanner misclassifications of argparse code and an auto-generated audit file. The 2 URL references point to the projects own GitHub repository. The skill is a standard, well-documented PDF processing tool with no malicious intent. No prompt injection attempts were detected.

content.user_title: Process PDF files offline - merge, split, extract
content.value_statement: Users often need to manipulate PDF files but lack reliable offline tools. This skill provides local PDF processing including merging, splitting, text and table extraction without any external services or cloud APIs.
actual_capabilities: Merge multiple PDF files into a single combined document
Split a PDF into individual page files for review or distribution
Extract specific page ranges from a PDF into a new PDF file
Extract selectable text from PDFs and save to plain text files
Extract tables from PDFs and save as CSV files for spreadsheet use
Create a PDF document from a plain text file
limitations: Does not support OCR; only selectable text in PDFs can be extracted
Table extraction quality depends on PDF structure; complex layouts may not reconstruct well
Cannot process scanned documents or image-based PDFs
Requires Python 3.9+ and specific PDF libraries to be installed locally

use_cases.title: Research paper organization
use_cases.description: A researcher needs to merge multiple PDF papers into one file for literature review and annotation.

use_cases.title: Document page extraction
use_cases.description: An office worker extracts specific pages from a large contract PDF to share relevant sections with colleagues.

use_cases.title: Data extraction from reports
use_cases.description: A data analyst extracts tables from quarterly report PDFs into CSV files for further analysis in spreadsheets.

prompt_templates.title: Merge PDFs
prompt_templates.prompt: Merge the PDF files report1.pdf and report2.pdf into a single file called combined.pdf
prompt_templates.scenario: Combine two PDF documents into one file

prompt_templates.title: Extract text from PDF
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract text from document.pdf and save it to document.txt
prompt_templates.scenario: Get plain text content from a PDF file

prompt_templates.title: Split PDF into pages
prompt_templates.prompt: Split the file large_document.pdf into individual page files in the pages_output directory
prompt_templates.scenario: Separate a multi-page PDF into single page files

prompt_templates.title: Extract tables to CSV
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all tables from financial_report.pdf and save them as CSV files in the tables_output directory
prompt_templates.scenario: Extract tabular data from PDF for spreadsheet use

output_examples.input: Merge a.pdf and b.pdf into merged.pdf
output_examples.output: Created merged.pdf with 15 pages (a.pdf: 8 pages + b.pdf: 7 pages)

output_examples.input: Extract pages 2-4 from document.pdf into excerpt.pdf
output_examples.output: Created excerpt.pdf with 3 pages (pages 2, 3, and 4 from document.pdf)

output_examples.input: Extract text from report.pdf into report.txt
output_examples.output: Saved extracted text to report.txt with content from 12 pages

best_practices: Verify that your PDFs contain selectable text before requesting extraction; scanned documents without OCR will produce empty results
Specify exact page ranges to avoid processing unnecessary pages and to get faster results
Use clear output file names with appropriate extensions (.pdf, .txt, .csv) to avoid format confusion
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to edit or modify existing PDF content; this tool only creates new files from source PDFs
Do not use this skill for image-based PDFs expecting text extraction; OCR is not supported
Do not request operations on encrypted or password-protected PDFs without first decrypting them

faq.question: Does this skill work with scanned PDF documents?
faq.answer: No. Only PDFs with selectable text content can be processed. Scanned documents require OCR which this skill does not support.

faq.question: Can I edit text inside a PDF using this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill only creates new files through operations like merging, splitting, extracting, and converting. It cannot modify existing PDF content directly.

faq.question: Is my data sent to any external service?
faq.answer: No. All processing happens locally on your machine using Python libraries. No data is sent over the network.

faq.question: What Python libraries are required?
faq.answer: You need pypdf, pdfplumber, and reportlab. Install them with: pip install -r scripts/requirements.txt

faq.question: Can I extract images from a PDF?
faq.answer: No. This skill can extract text and tables but not images from PDF documents.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: PDF for document operations, TXT for text extraction, and CSV for table extraction.
